# hrobot_ssh_key (Resource)

Manages an SSH public key in the Hetzner Robot key store. The key is identified by its server-computed fingerprint, which is the resource ID. Only the name can be changed in place; replacing the key material forces a new key.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "hrobot_ssh_key" "lab" {
  name       = "k3s-lab"
  public_key = file("~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub")
}

output "lab_key_fingerprint" {
  value = hrobot_ssh_key.lab.fingerprint
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Human-readable name for the key. Can be changed in place.
- `public_key` (String) The SSH public key in OpenSSH or SSH2 format. Changing this forces a new key, because the fingerprint changes.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) When the key was created in Robot.
- `fingerprint` (String) The server-computed fingerprint of the key.
- `id` (String) The key fingerprint, used as the resource ID and import ID.
- `size` (Number) The key size in bits.
- `type` (String) The key algorithm (e.g. ED25519, RSA, ECDSA).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import hrobot_ssh_key.lab '56:29:99:a4:5d:ed:ac:95:c1:f5:88:82:90:5d:dd:10'
```
